title: Freedom Score
subtitle: Data by [Freedom House](#dod:freedom-house). The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.
caption:
Freedom Score, 2025

Data by Freedom House. The score captures the extent of free and fair elections, political pluralism and participation, a functioning government, freedom of expression and association, the rule of law, and personal autonomy. Higher scores indicate more freedom.

This choropleth map displays the 2025 Freedom Score for countries worldwide, using a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow (lowest scores, near 0) to deep navy blue (highest scores, up to 101). The highest values, represented by the darkest blue shade, are concentrated across North America (United States, Canada), Western and Northern Europe (including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and the Nordic countries), Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and South Korea. Countries in the mid-to-high range, shown in medium blue, include much of South America (such as Brazil, Argentina, Chile), India, and South Africa. Lighter green and yellow-green shades, indicating scores roughly between 40 and 60, cover large parts of Africa (like Nigeria, Kenya, Ethiopia), Southeast Asia (Indonesia, Philippines), and Mexico. The lowest scores, depicted in pale yellow, are visible across much of the Middle East (Saudi Arabia, Iran), Central Asia, and parts of East Asia (China). Greenland and a few other territories are marked with diagonal hatching, signifying no available data. The geographic distribution shows a clear concentration of higher freedom scores in established democracies across the Americas, Europe, and Oceania, while lower scores cluster in regions with authoritarian governance or significant political restrictions. The map visually separates countries into distinct bands of freedom based on the composite score derived from multiple political rights and civil liberties indicators.
